content.text: A missing `local_dir` returns `ErrSourceMissing` — so test #2 (line 519) goes through the *error* path and currently asserts deletion. That means bug #1's fix directly conflicts with two existing tests that codify the current behavior. This is a real design fork. Let me check what deletion-control knobs the engine already exposes before deciding the fix shape.

content.text: The engine exposes `ExpectDeletions *int` — a pre-mutation guard that aborts if orphan deletions would occur. That opens a surgical fix option for bug #1. Let me confirm the trust plumbing for bug #2 (how the write-scope path loads revocation state) so I frame the contract question accurately.

input.command: grep -rn "func loadTrustState\|StateLoaded\|func Decide\|type DecideInput" internal/cli/*.go internal/trust/*.go | grep -v _test | head -20
input.description: Find trust state loading and Decide signature

content.content:
100	//
101	//  1. Revoke is absolute — even a TTY never overrides it.
102	//  2. ManifestTrustedSHA is authoritative when present: match proceeds,
103	//     mismatch either fails closed (default posture) or proceeds through
104	//     the explicit allow-new-shas posture when the caller also proved a
105	//     fast-forward.
106	//  3. User history is consulted next. Known+same proceeds. Known+new
107	//     either auto-promotes (AllowNewSHAs with active cooldown) or yields
108	//     KindProceedWithReminder (plan decision #9: no mid-sync prompt).
109	//  4. Unknown URL: CI needs --accept-new-source (exact SHA or =any+peer
110	//     gate); TTY gets KindPromptFirstURL.
111	func Decide(in DecideInput) (Decision, error) {
112		d := Decision{
113			URL:         in.URL,
114			ResolvedSHA: in.ResolvedSHA,
115			TrustedSHA:  effectiveTrustedSHA(in),
116		}
117	
118		// 1. Revoke beats everything.
119		if in.State.Revoked {
120			d.Kind = KindRevokedBlock
121			return d, fmt.Errorf("%w: %s", ErrRevokedTrustAnchor, in.URL)
122		}
123	
124		// 1b. The allow-new-shas auto path is fail-closed without loaded trust
125		// state. A zero State has Revoked==false, so proceeding here would skip
126		// the revoke check above. Refuse structurally rather than trusting the
127		// caller to have Folded the store.
128		if in.Posture == PostureAllowNewSHAs && !in.StateLoaded {
129			d.Kind = KindDecisionRequired
130			return d, fmt.Errorf("%w: allow-new-shas posture requires loaded trust state", ErrTrustDecisionRequired)
131		}
132	
133		// 2. Manifest pin authoritative when set.
134		if in.ManifestTrustedSHA != "" {
135			if in.ManifestTrustedSHA == in.ResolvedSHA {
136				d.Kind = KindProceed
137				return d, nil
138			}
139			if in.Posture == PostureAllowNewSHAs && in.FastForward {
140				d.Kind = KindProceedAutoAdvance
141				d.TrustedSHA = in.ResolvedSHA
142				d.AppendPending = PendingEntry{
143					TS:     in.Now,
144					TSRaw:  in.Now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
145					URL:    in.URL,
146					NewSHA: in.ResolvedSHA,
147					OldSHA: in.ManifestTrustedSHA,
148				}
149				return d, nil
150			}
151			// Pin mismatch: CI fails closed. For a TTY, sync still uses the pin
152			// and emits a reminder so the user notices a newer SHA exists —
153			// this matches plan decision #9's "sync no longer prompts mid-sync"
154			// rule and reuses the pending-review flow.
155			if !in.TTY {
156				d.Kind = KindDecisionRequired
157				d.Remediation = fmt.Sprintf(
158					"trusted_sha (%s) does not match resolved SHA (%s). "+
159						"Run `agent-sync trust promote %s --pin-manifest` to accept, or "+
160						"update canonical.commit to match.",
161					shortSHA(in.ManifestTrustedSHA), shortSHA(in.ResolvedSHA), in.URL,
162				)
163				return d, fmt.Errorf("%w: manifest pin %s != resolved %s",
164					ErrTrustDecisionRequired, shortSHA(in.ManifestTrustedSHA), shortSHA(in.ResolvedSHA))
165			}
166			// TTY + pin + drift → reminder + pending, using the pin.
167			d.Kind = KindProceedWithReminder
168			d.Reminder = fmt.Sprintf(
169				"Newer SHA available for %s: %s (currently pinned to %s). "+
170					"Run `agent-sync trust pending` to review.",
171				in.URL, shortSHA(in.ResolvedSHA), shortSHA(in.ManifestTrustedSHA),
172			)
173			d.AppendPending = PendingEntry{
174				TS:     in.Now,
175				TSRaw:  in.Now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
176				URL:    in.URL,
177				NewSHA: in.ResolvedSHA,
178				OldSHA: in.ManifestTrustedSHA,
179			}

content.text:
I now have the full picture of both bugs:

**Bug #1** — `materializeLayerReadOnly` failures are swallowed with `Warn`+`continue`, so a failed ancestor layer drops out of the resolved set and the engine deletes its previously-inherited paths. Two existing tests (`hierarchy_sync_test.go:187` and `:519`) currently codify this delete-on-failure behavior, so fixing it means rewriting their expectations.

**Bug #2** — `materializeURL` (line 179) calls `trust.Decide` with no loaded `State`, so revocation is never consulted on the pinned emission path. But this is true of *both* the write scope and ancestors today — the auto-advance path (`setup.go:409`) is the only place that Folds the store. So whether this is even a bug depends on the intended contract.

Both are genuine forks the handoff explicitly left for a decision. Let me get your call before implementing.

questions.question: Bug #1: when an inherited (ancestor) layer can't be materialized this run, and emitting the write scope would delete already-inherited files, what should agent-sync do?

options.label: Refuse only if it'd delete
options.description: Proceed normally when no inherited files would be lost (fresh sync still emits its own content); hard-fail the scope with a clear error + non-zero exit only when emission would delete inherited content. Non-destructive, loud exactly when data is at risk. Implemented via the engine's existing ExpectDeletions=0 guard.

options.label: Always defer the scope
options.description: Skip the write scope entirely whenever any ancestor fails (mirrors the cursor D8 guard), warn, exit 0. Never deletes, but also skips the scope's own-content updates and stays quiet (exit 0).

options.label: Hard-fail on any ancestor failure
options.description: Any unreadable ancestor fails the write scope loudly (non-zero exit), even on a fresh sync where nothing would be lost. Simplest, most conservative, noisiest.

questions.question: Bug #2: what should a REVOKED trust anchor block? (Today, revoke only blocks the auto-advance path; already-pinned content is emitted regardless, for both the write scope and inherited layers.)

options.label: Advancement/TOFU only (keep today)
options.description: Revoke prevents accepting new SHAs and new sources, but already-pinned commits keep being emitted everywhere. Ancestors already behave this way — resolution is to add a test documenting the contract, no behavior change. Lowest blast radius.

options.label: Block emission too
options.description: A revoked anchor stops delivering ANY content, including already-pinned commits, through both the write and inherited paths — a hard kill-switch. Makes ancestor layers consult the revocation log. Larger change; a revoke can break pinned syncs across every repo that pins that source until the source is removed from the manifest.

(Today, revoke only blocks the auto-advance path; already-pinned content is emitted regardless, for both the write scope and inherited layers.)"="Advancement/TOFU only (keep today)". content.text:
Decisions locked in:
- **Bug #1**: *Refuse only if it'd delete* — proceed when safe, hard-fail (non-zero exit, clear error) only when emission would delete inherited content. Mechanism: the engine's `ExpectDeletions=0` guard.
- **Bug #2**: *Advancement/TOFU only* — no behavior change; add a test documenting the contract.

`CheckExpectedDeletions` returns `ErrDeletionCountMismatch` *before* any mutation (target.go:627 precedes the write loop) — exactly the data-preserving abort I want. This confirms the "refuse only if it'd delete" design works cleanly:

- **Fresh sync + failed ancestor** → 0 orphans → passes the guard → emits own content (so existing test #1 at line 187 keeps passing unchanged).
- **Previously-inherited content + failed ancestor** → orphans > 0 → aborts before mutation → content preserved, scope errors (so test #2 at line 519 flips to asserting preservation + error).

content.content:
1	package cli
2	
3	import "errors"
4	
5	// Documented process exit codes. Operational failures use exitError
6	// (from cmd_adapter.go) or these constants; the report verdict carries
7	// its own 0/1 in report.Outcome.ExitCode.
8	const (
9		exitOK      = 0 // success
10		exitFailure = 1 // operation completed but reported failure/drift
11		exitUsage   = 2 // usage error, missing required flag, spawn failure
12	)
13	
14	// exitCoder is any error that carries its own process exit code (the
15	// trust package, the adapter exitError, MissingFlagError all implement
16	// it). MapExit unwraps the chain looking for one.
17	type exitCoder interface{ ExitCode() int }
18	
19	// MapExit translates an error returned from command execution into a
20	// process exit code. nil → 0. An error implementing ExitCode() (anywhere
21	// in its Unwrap chain) uses that code. Everything else is a generic
22	// failure (exitUsage), since an unclassified error at the command
23	// boundary is almost always a usage/operational problem rather than a
24	// clean "operation reported failure" signal.
25	func MapExit(err error) int {
26		if err == nil {
27			return exitOK
28		}
29		var ec exitCoder
30		if errors.As(err, &ec) {
31			code := ec.ExitCode()
32			// An ExitCode() of 0 on a non-nil error is a contradiction; treat
33			// it as a generic failure rather than silently reporting success.
34			if code == 0 {
35				return exitUsage
36			}
37			return code
38		}
39		return exitUsage
40	}
41

content.text:
The debug is illuminating — and it reveals the real subtlety. On a descendant sync, the project ancestor is **forced offline** (`materializeLayerReadOnly`: `offline := m.Canonical.URL != ""`), so with a cold cache it *always* fails with `ErrUnresolvablePinOffline`. That's precisely bug #1's trigger. But it also means my blunt `ExpectDeletions=0` would over-refuse: a chronically-cold URL ancestor (that never actually contributed) would block the descendant's *own* legitimate deletions.

I need to impose the guard only when the failed ancestor **actually contributed** previously-emitted content. Let me check whether the ledger records per-path source provenance.

content.text: The guard *works* — inherited content is preserved and exit is non-zero. But the refusal surfaces via the summary (target failure, exit 1), not `out.Err`, because in atomic mode the engine records the deletion-guard failure in the per-target status and returns `nil`. So the bare "deletion count differs" message reaches the user instead of my explanatory one. Let me check whether the engine exposes a pre-sync plan so I can refuse cleanly *before* Sync with a good message:

content.text: `engine.Plan` exists and returns `WouldDelete` per change. That's a much cleaner mechanism than the `ExpectDeletions` hack: when a failed ancestor exists, plan first, and refuse with a clear error *before* Sync only if something would actually be deleted. Let me read `Plan`/`PlanResult`:

`Plan` gives me per-target `WouldDelete` up front. That's cleaner than the `ExpectDeletions` override — a deterministic read-only pre-check that refuses with a clear message *before* Sync, leaves the user's flag untouched, and lets safe (zero-deletion) runs proceed. input.prompt:
Adversarially review an uncommitted change on branch `fix/hierarchy-inheritance-followups` in the agent-sync repo (Go). Your job is to BREAK it — construct concrete failure scenarios, don't just confirm it looks fine.

## Context
This fixes two pre-existing bugs in the #43 harness-hierarchy inheritance model. In `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go`, `runHierarchySync` discovers a scope hierarchy (user → workspace → project → directory), selects ONE write scope (the closest non-user, or user under --user), and materializes every OTHER discovered scope as a read-only ancestor "layer" merged into the write scope's node set via last-write-wins (`applyResolvedLayers` → `harness.ResolveNodes`). Ancestor layers are materialized OFFLINE (`materializeLayerReadOnly` forces `offline := m.Canonical.URL != ""`), so a URL-sourced ancestor with a cold/evicted cache fails to materialize.

**Bug #1 (the fix):** Previously, an ancestor that failed to materialize was swallowed (`Warn` + `continue`) and dropped from the resolved layer set. The engine (`internal/engine/plan.go` / `target.go`) then treated the dropped ancestor's previously-emitted inherited paths as orphan deletions → SILENT deletion of inherited content, exit 0. The fix: track failed ancestors (`failedLayers`), and for the write scope, if any ancestor it inherits from failed (`firstFailedAncestor`), run `engine.Plan` first (`refuseIfWouldDelete`); if the plan would delete ANY already-managed file, REFUSE the scope (set `out.Err`, skip `engine.Sync`) so nothing is written or deleted. If the plan deletes nothing (fresh scope, or ancestor never contributed), proceed to Sync normally.

**Bug #2:** just a contract-documenting test — a revoked trust anchor gates advancement/TOFU, NOT emission of already-pinned content.

## The decision constraints (do NOT relitigate these; review correctness WITHIN them)
- Chosen behavior: "refuse only if it'd delete" (proceed when zero deletions; hard-fail when deletion would occur). Not full defer, not blanket hard-fail.
- The ledger has NO per-path source provenance, so a would-be deletion cannot be attributed to the failed ancestor specifically — over-refusal (blocking the scope's OWN legit deletion when an ancestor is also broken) is a known, accepted tradeoff.

## What I need you to hunt for
1. **Plan/Sync divergence**: Can `engine.Plan`'s `WouldDelete` disagree with what `engine.Sync` actually deletes, such that Plan says 0 but Sync deletes inherited content (the exact bug reappearing)? Check `internal/engine/plan.go` vs `internal/engine/target.go` deletion computation (owned-subdir, shared-leaf/sibling handling).
2. **firstFailedAncestor correctness**: does it correctly identify the write scope's ancestors given discovery ordering and the `layersForScope` traversal it mirrors? Off-by-one, wrong scope matched, user-scope edge cases (--user selects the user scope as write scope).
3. **Auto-advance interaction**: the write scope may have advanced its own pin (`prep.PinMovedTo`) before the refusal; I wrap with `pinMovedSyncError`. Any remaining inconsistency (pin moved on disk, sync refused)?
4. **Registry/root/lock preconditions for `engine.Plan`** — is `req` fully valid at the Plan call site? Could Plan panic or error spuriously?
5. **Any path where inherited content can STILL be silently deleted** despite the fix (e.g., a scope that isn't the write scope, cursor composition deferral interaction, best-effort vs atomic mode, multiple failed ancestors, a failed ancestor that is ALSO the write scope).
6. Exit-code correctness (refusal must be non-zero).

Read `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go` (esp. `runHierarchySync`, `firstFailedAncestor`, `refuseIfWouldDelete`, `materializeLayerReadOnly`, `applyResolvedLayers`, `layersForScope`), `internal/engine/plan.go`, `internal/engine/target.go` (deletion logic), `internal/engine/engine.go` (`Plan`, `Sync`), and the new tests in `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go`. The working diff is at /tmp/branch.diff.

Report concrete, ranked findings with a failure scenario for each (inputs → wrong outcome). Distinguish CONFIRMED (you traced the code path) from PLAUSIBLE. If you find nothing exploitable within the decision constraints, say so plainly rather than inventing style nits.

content.text:
Exit codes confirmed non-zero for both refusal branches (`exitUsage` for a plain refusal, `exitUpdatePinMoved` for the pin-moved wrap). Exit-code correctness holds.

I have traced enough to report. Here are my findings.

```json
{
  "reviewer": "adversarial",
  "findings": [
    {
      "title": "Guard bypass: refuseIfWouldDelete ignores per-target plan errors, so a plan that fails before its deletion loop reports 0 deletions and Sync silently deletes inherited content",
      "confidence": 65,
      "severity": "high",
      "location": "internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go:306-325 (refuseIfWouldDelete); internal/engine/engine.go:233-254 (Plan); internal/engine/plan.go:22-181 (planTarget)",
      "autofix_class": "advisory",
      "owner": "human",
      "evidence": [
        "The fix's safety hinges on refuseIfWouldDelete: it calls engine.Plan and refuses only if perr != nil OR sum(WouldDelete) > 0. Its comment asserts 'A failed plan is itself a refusal.' That is only true for the top-level error return of engine.Plan.",
        "engine.Plan (engine.go:246-252) calls `change := planTarget(...)` — planTarget returns a TargetChange with NO error channel. Plan appends the change and sets DriftDetected purely from the four slices. It NEVER inspects change.Error. Given a valid Root/Registry, engine.Plan therefore returns (result, nil) unconditionally — perr in refuseIfWouldDelete is effectively always nil.",
        "planTarget records per-target failures in change.Error and returns EARLY, at multiple points that all precede its deletion loop (plan.go:159-173): loadLedger (28), runAdapter (38), DryMerge on tool-owned (89), sibling-ledger load (59), DryNativeMerge (117), and the out-of-band readHash real-I/O error (153). On any of these, change.WouldDelete is left empty.",
        "Chain: failed ancestor drops inherited paths from the resolved node set -> those paths become orphan deletions -> for the affected target planTarget errors BEFORE the deletion loop (e.g. an EACCES/EIO reading one already-managed, ledger-clean file at plan.go:141) -> change.Error is set, change.WouldDelete = [] -> engine.Plan swallows change.Error -> refuseIfWouldDelete sums 0 deletions and returns nil -> the guard PROCEEDS -> engine.Sync/applyTarget runs, computes the same orphan set (target.go:603-621) and DeleteOrphans (781) removes the inherited content. Exit 0. The exact silent-deletion bug the fix targets.",
        "The out-of-band readHash check (plan.go:141-155) is the cleanest divergence: it exists ONLY in planTarget. applyTarget has no equivalent on-disk read for ledger-clean paths, so an I/O error there aborts Plan's accounting but never blocks Sync's deletion. DryMerge (plan.go:86) and DryNativeMerge (plan.go:115) are additional divergence surfaces — a dry-run that errors while the real ApplyToFile/ApplyNativeToFile succeeds would also yield Plan=0/Sync-deletes (though those merges run before DeleteOrphans in applyTarget, so a symmetric failure aborts safely; only asymmetric dry-vs-real failures leak)."
      ],
      "why_not_suppressed": "Every step except the concrete I/O-error trigger is mechanically verifiable from the diff and the two deletion routines. The design gap — refuseIfWouldDelete has zero visibility into per-target plan errors despite claiming a failed plan refuses — holds regardless of trigger frequency, and the readHash path is a confirmed Plan-only code path that does not gate Sync.",
      "suggested_fix": "In refuseIfWouldDelete, treat any non-empty plan.Targets[i].Error as a refusal (same as perr != nil), OR have engine.Plan surface per-target errors as a real error. Otherwise the guard's 'cannot compute the plan -> refuse' invariant is not actually enforced."
    },
    {
      "title": "Plan/Sync deletion divergence under --adopt: the guard's dry-run never adopts, so Sync can adopt-then-orphan-delete paths the guard counted as 0",
      "confidence": 55,
      "severity": "medium",
      "location": "internal/engine/plan.go:159-173 (planTarget deletions, no adopt); internal/engine/target.go:462-489 (applyTarget adopt + ledger reload)",
      "autofix_class": "advisory",
      "owner": "human",
      "evidence": [
        "applyTarget, when drift is detected and the prefix is in Options.AdoptPrefixes (target.go:471-477), calls AdoptEntries and then RELOADS the ledger (target.go:484-489). The comment at 483-484 is explicit: 'an adopted-then-undesired file must still be detected as an orphan.' So adoption can ADD paths to the deletable set (target.go:603-621).",
        "planTarget has no adopt path at all — it reports drift as OutOfBand and computes WouldDelete from the un-adopted ledger only. So for a scope run with --adopt, Plan's WouldDelete can be strictly smaller than what Sync deletes.",
        "req.Options = opts.EngineOpts is copied into the request used for BOTH the guard's Plan and the subsequent Sync (hierarchy_sync.go:124), so AdoptPrefixes is live at the guard call site. Scenario: user runs `agent-sync sync --adopt` in a hierarchy with a failed ancestor; the guard's Plan reports 0 deletions for a prefix whose drift Sync will adopt and then orphan-delete -> guard proceeds -> Sync deletes.",
        "Caveat that lowers severity: adopted files are previously-UNMANAGED on-disk content, not agent-sync inherited content, so this does not reintroduce the specific inherited-content bug; it does mean the guard is not a faithful predictor of Sync's deletions whenever --adopt is in play."
      ],
      "why_not_suppressed": "The adopt-reload-then-orphan path in applyTarget and its total absence from planTarget are both directly visible; the divergence is constructible. Confidence is mid because the deleted paths are adopted user files rather than inherited agent-sync content, so the blast radius is narrower than finding 1."
    }
  ],
  "residual_risks": [
    "Auto-advance + refuse: when the write scope already advanced its own pin during prepare (prep.PinMovedTo) and the guard then refuses, the manifest pin is left ahead on disk while nothing was emitted. The code wraps the refusal in pinMovedSyncError so the operator is told, and this is within the stated decision constraints, but the on-disk manifest mutation is real and persists across the refusal — a subsequent successful run is required to reconcile. Confirmed via hierarchy_sync.go:155-159; acknowledged, not a new defect.",
    "Double adapter execution: the guard's engine.Plan runs every target's adapter session (NewSession/Initialize/Emit/Shutdown) and then engine.Sync runs them all again. runAdapter is documented read-only, so this is a cost/latency concern, not correctness — but any adapter with non-idempotent Initialize side effects would be invoked twice on the refuse-check path.",
    "Over-refusal (accepted per constraints): because the ledger has no per-path provenance, a write scope with a legitimate deletion of its OWN content that also has a failed ancestor is refused. Confirmed and explicitly accepted in the task constraints."
  ],
  "testing_gaps": [
    "The new test (TestRunHierarchySync_RefusesToDeleteInheritedContentWhenAncestorFails) covers only the happy refusal path: materialize fails -> Plan reports deletions -> refuse. There is no test for finding 1 — a planTarget that sets change.Error before its deletion loop (e.g. an unreadable managed file, or a DryMerge/DryNativeMerge failure) while inherited orphans exist. That is the exact condition under which the guard silently proceeds and Sync deletes; it is currently unguarded and untested.",
    "No test exercises the guard under --adopt (finding 2), best-effort mode with multiple targets where only some would delete, or multiple simultaneously-failed ancestors — the guard's whole-scope refusal semantics across those combinations are asserted only by construction, not by a test.",
    "No test asserts Plan/Sync deletion PARITY directly (that the count refuseIfWouldDelete sees equals what applyTarget's deletable set would remove for the same req). Such a parity test is the natural regression fence for finding 1 and any future drift between plan.go and target.go deletion logic."
  ]
}
```

## Context
This fixes two pre-existing bugs in the #43 harness-hierarchy inheritance model. In `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go`, `runHierarchySync` discovers a scope hierarchy (user → workspace → project → directory), selects ONE write scope (the closest non-user, or user under --user), and materializes every OTHER discovered scope as a read-only ancestor "layer" merged into the write scope's node set via last-write-wins (`applyResolvedLayers` → `harness.ResolveNodes`). Ancestor layers are materialized OFFLINE (`materializeLayerReadOnly` forces `offline := m.Canonical.URL != ""`), so a URL-sourced ancestor with a cold/evicted cache fails to materialize.

**Bug #1 (the fix):** Previously, an ancestor that failed to materialize was swallowed (`Warn` + `continue`) and dropped from the resolved layer set. The engine (`internal/engine/plan.go` / `target.go`) then treated the dropped ancestor's previously-emitted inherited paths as orphan deletions → SILENT deletion of inherited content, exit 0. The fix: track failed ancestors (`failedLayers`), and for the write scope, if any ancestor it inherits from failed (`firstFailedAncestor`), run `engine.Plan` first (`refuseIfWouldDelete`); if the plan would delete ANY already-managed file, REFUSE the scope (set `out.Err`, skip `engine.Sync`) so nothing is written or deleted. If the plan deletes nothing (fresh scope, or ancestor never contributed), proceed to Sync normally.

**Bug #2:** just a contract-documenting test — a revoked trust anchor gates advancement/TOFU, NOT emission of already-pinned content.

## The decision constraints (do NOT relitigate these; review correctness WITHIN them)
- Chosen behavior: "refuse only if it'd delete" (proceed when zero deletions; hard-fail when deletion would occur). Not full defer, not blanket hard-fail.
- The ledger has NO per-path source provenance, so a would-be deletion cannot be attributed to the failed ancestor specifically — over-refusal (blocking the scope's OWN legit deletion when an ancestor is also broken) is a known, accepted tradeoff.

## What I need you to hunt for
1. **Plan/Sync divergence**: Can `engine.Plan`'s `WouldDelete` disagree with what `engine.Sync` actually deletes, such that Plan says 0 but Sync deletes inherited content (the exact bug reappearing)? Check `internal/engine/plan.go` vs `internal/engine/target.go` deletion computation (owned-subdir, shared-leaf/sibling handling).
2. **firstFailedAncestor correctness**: does it correctly identify the write scope's ancestors given discovery ordering and the `layersForScope` traversal it mirrors? Off-by-one, wrong scope matched, user-scope edge cases (--user selects the user scope as write scope).
3. **Auto-advance interaction**: the write scope may have advanced its own pin (`prep.PinMovedTo`) before the refusal; I wrap with `pinMovedSyncError`. Any remaining inconsistency (pin moved on disk, sync refused)?
4. **Registry/root/lock preconditions for `engine.Plan`** — is `req` fully valid at the Plan call site? Could Plan panic or error spuriously?
5. **Any path where inherited content can STILL be silently deleted** despite the fix (e.g., a scope that isn't the write scope, cursor composition deferral interaction, best-effort vs atomic mode, multiple failed ancestors, a failed ancestor that is ALSO the write scope).
6. Exit-code correctness (refusal must be non-zero).

Read `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go` (esp. `runHierarchySync`, `firstFailedAncestor`, `refuseIfWouldDelete`, `materializeLayerReadOnly`, `applyResolvedLayers`, `layersForScope`), `internal/engine/plan.go`, `internal/engine/target.go` (deletion logic), `internal/engine/engine.go` (`Plan`, `Sync`), and the new tests in `internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go`. The working diff is at /tmp/branch.diff.

Report concrete, ranked findings with a failure scenario for each (inputs → wrong outcome). Distinguish CONFIRMED (you traced the code path) from PLAUSIBLE. If you find nothing exploitable within the decision constraints, say so plainly rather than inventing style nits.

The adversarial review found a **real high-severity gap** in my fix (finding #1). Let me verify it: `engine.Plan` swallows per-target failures into `change.Error` and returns `(result, nil)` — so my `refuseIfWouldDelete` comment "a failed plan is itself a refusal" is false for per-target errors. If `planTarget` errors *before* its deletion loop (e.g. out-of-band `readHash` I/O error) while inherited content would be orphaned, `WouldDelete` is empty, `perr` is nil → my guard proceeds → Sync deletes. That reintroduces the exact bug.

Let me confirm `planTarget`'s early-return-before-deletion-loop paths:

Confirmed — the test reproduces finding #1 (fails without the `ch.Error` check, passes with it). The regression fence is genuine.

Now finding #2 (`--adopt` divergence): I traced it further. The guard's purpose is protecting *inherited* (already-managed, ledger-tracked) content, which `planTarget`'s deletion loop counts faithfully — so Plan never *under*-counts inherited orphans (adopt only *adds* adopted-file deletions on top). The safety property holds; the divergence only affects the count of newly-adopted unmanaged files, which is normal `--adopt` behavior. I'll document this bound rather than replicate adopt logic in the pre-check for no safety gain:

content.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [Unreleased]

### Changed

- **BREAKING (semver-major): `agent-sync sync` auto-advances the canonical pin by
  default.** Previously `sync` materialized the pinned `commit` and never moved it;
  advancing required the explicit, gated `agent-sync update`. Now `sync` resolves
  the newest upstream commit on the manifest's `ref`, and — for git-backed sources
  that are pinned and carry a `trusted_sha` — advances the pin and re-pins
  `commit` + `trusted_sha` forward automatically, auto-accepting the trust
  decision **through the trust policy engine**. Safety rails: advancement is
  **fast-forward-only** (rewritten/force-pushed history is refused, pin kept); a
  **revoked trust anchor always refuses**; **offline / unreachable falls back to
  the cached pin** with a warning (never errors); every advance is recorded to the
  structured log and the cache audit file. `local_dir` sources are unaffected
  (nothing to pin); `local_path` sources advance fast-forward-only. Applies per
  scope across the user → workspace → project/directory hierarchy.

  **Migration.** On upgrade, existing pinned workspaces begin tracking upstream on
  the next `sync`. To keep today's deterministic, human-gated behavior:
  - pass `--frozen` for a single run, or
  - set `canonical.auto: false` in `.agent-sync.yaml` to opt a workspace out
    permanently (then advance deliberately with `agent-sync update`).

  CI and any reproducibility-sensitive pipeline should use one of the opt-outs.
  See `docs/threat-model.md` (T1) for the revised supply-chain posture: human

## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-07

### Added

toolUseResult.stdout:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [Unreleased]

### Changed

- **BREAKING (semver-major): `agent-sync sync` auto-advances the canonical pin by
  default.** Previously `sync` materialized the pinned `commit` and never moved it;
  advancing required the explicit, gated `agent-sync update`. Now `sync` resolves
  the newest upstream commit on the manifest's `ref`, and — for git-backed sources
  that are pinned and carry a `trusted_sha` — advances the pin and re-pins
  `commit` + `trusted_sha` forward automatically, auto-accepting the trust
  decision **through the trust policy engine**. Safety rails: advancement is
  **fast-forward-only** (rewritten/force-pushed history is refused, pin kept); a
  **revoked trust anchor always refuses**; **offline / unreachable falls back to
  the cached pin** with a warning (never errors); every advance is recorded to the
  structured log and the cache audit file. `local_dir` sources are unaffected
  (nothing to pin); `local_path` sources advance fast-forward-only. Applies per
  scope across the user → workspace → project/directory hierarchy.

  **Migration.** On upgrade, existing pinned workspaces begin tracking upstream on
  the next `sync`. To keep today's deterministic, human-gated behavior:
  - pass `--frozen` for a single run, or
  - set `canonical.auto: false` in `.agent-sync.yaml` to opt a workspace out
    permanently (then advance deliberately with `agent-sync update`).

  CI and any reproducibility-sensitive pipeline should use one of the opt-outs.
  See `docs/threat-model.md` (T1) for the revised supply-chain posture: human
  review is no longer in the default advance path, so fast-forward-only and the
  trust policy are the remaining rails, and commit-signature verification is still
  absent.

## [Unreleased]

### Fixed

- **Hierarchy sync no longer silently deletes inherited content when an ancestor
  layer can't be materialized.** When a read-only ancestor scope failed to
  materialize this run (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the
  offline-forced ancestor cache), its contribution silently dropped out of the
  write scope's resolved set and the engine deleted the previously-inherited
  paths (exit 0). The write scope now dry-runs first and **refuses** — preserving
  the inherited files and surfacing a non-zero, explanatory error — whenever
  proceeding would delete already-managed content or the plan can't be computed.
  A fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually contributed, still syncs its
  own content unaffected. (#43 inheritance follow-up.)

### Changed

diff --git a/internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go b/internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go

--- a/internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go
+++ b/internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go
@@ -74,9 +74,19 @@ func runHierarchySync(ctx context.Context, rc *runtimeContext, cwd, home string,
 	scopes = selectWriteScopes(scopes, opts.IncludeUser)
 
 	preparedLayers := make([]preparedLayer, 0, len(scopes))
+	// failedLayers records the ancestors that could NOT be materialized this
+	// run (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the
+	// offline-forced ancestor cache). A dropped ancestor is not merely a lost
+	// warning: the write scope inherits from every ancestor, so its resolved
+	// node set would silently omit that ancestor's contribution and the engine
+	// would treat previously-inherited paths as orphan deletions. The emit loop
+	// consults this to refuse rather than silently under-emit — see the
+	// zero-deletion guard below.
+	failedLayers := make(map[string]error)
 	for _, sc := range scopes {
 		pl, lerr := materializeLayerReadOnly(ctx, rc, sc, now)
 		if lerr != nil {
+			failedLayers[sc.ManifestPath] = lerr
 			if rc != nil && rc.Logger != nil {
 				rc.Logger.Warn("harness: cannot materialize scope", "path", sc.ManifestPath, "err", lerr)
 			}
@@ -114,6 +124,7 @@ func runHierarchySync(ctx context.Context, rc *runtimeContext, cwd, home string,
 			req.Options = opts.EngineOpts
 			req.Options.RunLockHeld = prep.RunLockHeld
 			applyResolvedLayers(&req, sc, scopes, preparedLayers)
+			failedAncestorPath, failedAncestorErr := firstFailedAncestor(sc, scopes, failedLayers)
 			// Fold the user-scope Cursor rule layer into this project scope's node
 			// set (plan U4/D1/D2), via the shared entry point also used by the
 			// single-scope path.
@@ -123,6 +134,32 @@ func runHierarchySync(ctx context.Context, rc *runtimeContext, cwd, home string,
 			// level. Computed after a successful prepare (nodes exist);
 			// independent of the sync outcome.
 			out.Warnings = coverage.Analyze(sc.Level, kindsOf(req.Nodes), req.Targets)
+			// Data-loss guard (#43 inheritance follow-up): if an ancestor layer
+			// this scope inherits from failed to materialize this run (malformed
+			// manifest, or a pin absent from the offline-forced ancestor cache),
+			// the resolved node set is missing that ancestor's contribution.
+			// Emitting anyway would let the engine delete the previously-inherited
+			// paths that dropped out. Dry-run first: if nothing would be deleted
+			// (a fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually contributed),
+			// proceed normally; otherwise refuse BEFORE any mutation so the
+			// inherited content is preserved and the operator sees the real cause.
+			// The ledger records no per-path source, so a would-be deletion cannot
+			// be attributed to the dropped ancestor specifically — this errs
+			// toward refusing over silently dropping inherited content.
+			if failedAncestorPath != "" {
+				if refusal := refuseIfWouldDelete(ctx, req, sc, failedAncestorPath, failedAncestorErr); refusal != nil {
+					// A refusal after this scope auto-advanced its own pin must
+					// still surface that the manifest moved (matching the
+					// sync-error path below), so the operator knows the pin is
+					// ahead of what was emitted.
+					if prep.PinMovedTo != "" {
+						out.Err = pinMovedSyncError(prep.PinMovedTo, refusal)
+					} else {
+						out.Err = refusal
+					}
+					return out
+				}
+			}
 			summary, serr := engine.Sync(ctx, req)
 			if serr != nil {
 				if prep.PinMovedTo != "" {
@@ -241,6 +278,70 @@ func layersForScope(sc hierarchy.Scope, scopes []hierarchy.Scope, preparedLayers
 	return out
 }
 
+// firstFailedAncestor reports the manifest path and error of the first ancestor
+// (read-only) layer of sc that failed to materialize this run, or "" if every
+// ancestor materialized. Ancestors are the scopes discovered before sc; this
+// mirrors layersForScope's traversal (stop at sc's own manifest) so the notion
+// of "which layers this scope inherits from" stays identical between resolution
+// and the data-loss guard. sc's own manifest and any scope after it are not
+// ancestors and are ignored.
+func firstFailedAncestor(sc hierarchy.Scope, scopes []hierarchy.Scope, failed map[string]error) (string, error) {
+	for _, discovered := range scopes {
+		if discovered.ManifestPath == sc.ManifestPath {
+			return "", nil
+		}
+		if err, ok := failed[discovered.ManifestPath]; ok {
+			return discovered.ManifestPath, err
+		}
+	}
+	return "", nil
+}
+
+// refuseIfWouldDelete dry-runs req and returns a data-loss refusal error when
+// emitting would delete any already-managed path, given that ancestorPath (an
+// inherited layer) failed to materialize this run. It returns nil ONLY when the
+// plan is fully computed AND deletes nothing, so a fresh scope or a
+// never-contributing ancestor still syncs its own content.
+//
+// A plan that cannot be computed is itself a refusal. engine.Plan records a
+// per-target failure in TargetChange.Error and still returns a nil top-level
+// error, and several of those failures (ledger load, adapter run, dry-merge,
+// the out-of-band readHash) return BEFORE planTarget's deletion loop — leaving
+// WouldDelete empty. Trusting a zero count from such a plan would let the very
+// deletion this guard exists to prevent slip through, so any per-target error
+// is treated as "cannot prove it is safe" and refuses.
+//
+// Scope: this protects already-managed (ledger-tracked) content, which the plan
+// counts identically to sync. Under --adopt, sync also deletes newly-adopted,
+// previously-UNMANAGED files that the plan does not model — but that is ordinary
+// --adopt churn, never inherited content, so the plan can only OVER-count, never
+// under-count, the deletions this guard cares about.
+func refuseIfWouldDelete(ctx context.Context, req engine.Request, sc hierarchy.Scope, ancestorPath string, ancestorErr error) error {
+	plan, perr := engine.Plan(ctx, req)
+	if perr != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf(
+			"refusing to sync %s: inherited layer %s could not be materialized this run (%s) and the resulting plan could not be computed: %w",
+			sc.Level, ancestorPath, ancestorErr.Error(), perr)
+	}
+	var wouldDelete int
+	for _, ch := range plan.Targets {
+		if ch.Error != "" {
+			return fmt.Errorf(
+				"refusing to sync %s: inherited layer %s could not be materialized this run (%s), and the plan for target %q could not be computed (%s), so it cannot be proven safe; restore or re-fetch that source (e.g. sync its scope online once), then re-run",
+				sc.Level, ancestorPath, ancestorErr.Error(), ch.Target, ch.Error)
+		}
+		wouldDelete += len(ch.WouldDelete)
+	}
+	if wouldDelete == 0 {
+		return nil
+	}
+	return fmt.Errorf(
+		"refusing to sync %s: inherited layer %s could not be materialized this run (%v), "+
+			"and proceeding would delete %d already-managed file(s) that may be inherited content; "+
+			"restore or re-fetch that source (e.g. sync its scope online once), then re-run",
+		sc.Level, ancestorPath, ancestorErr, wouldDelete)
+}
+
 func layerFromPrepared(pl preparedLayer) harness.Layer {
 	return harness.Layer{
 		Scope:     pl.Scope.Level.String(),

content.text:
The implementation is complete and verified. Here's where things stand:

## Summary

Both #43 inheritance follow-ups from the handoff are done, on branch `fix/hierarchy-inheritance-followups`:

**Bug #1 — silent inherited-content drop (fixed).** When a read-only ancestor layer fails to materialize (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the offline-forced ancestor cache), the write scope now **dry-runs first and refuses** rather than silently deleting the dropped ancestor's previously-inherited paths. Per your "refuse only if it'd delete" choice: a fresh scope or a never-contributing ancestor still syncs its own content; a run that *would* delete already-managed content aborts before any mutation with a non-zero, explanatory error.

**Bug #2 — revoked ancestor (documented).** Per your "advancement/TOFU-only" choice: no behavior change. Added `TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent` pinning the contract that a revoked anchor gates advancement, not pinned emission.

**Adversarial review caught a real high-severity gap in my first cut** — `engine.Plan` swallows per-target failures into `TargetChange.Error` and returns a nil top-level error, so a `planTarget` that errored *before* its deletion loop (e.g. out-of-band `readHash`) would have reported `WouldDelete=0` and let the exact bug slip through a different trigger. Fixed (`refuseIfWouldDelete` treats any per-target plan error as a refusal) and fenced with `TestRunHierarchySync_RefusesWhenPlanCannotBeComputedForFailedAncestor` (verified: fails without the fix, passes with it). Finding #2 (`--adopt` divergence) traced to *not* affect inherited content — documented in-code.

**Gates:** `go vet` clean · `go test -race ./...` green · `golangci-lint` 0 issues · coverage 80.5% (floor 80.0%). CHANGELOG stamped under Unreleased.

input.command: grep -n "^func TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent\|^func TestHierarchySyncEmitsCoverageWarning\|^// TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent" internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go

content.content:
682	// TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent is the
683	// #43 inheritance follow-up (bug #2): it documents the trust contract that a
684	// REVOKED anchor gates ADVANCEMENT / trust-on-first-use, NOT emission of
685	// already-pinned content. An ancestor (read-only) layer whose canonical source
686	// has a revoked anchor but an on-disk pin still delivers its pinned content into
687	// the descendant write scope through inheritance. This mirrors the write-scope
688	// pinned-emission path, which has never consulted the revocation log either.
689	func TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent(t *testing.T) {
690		requireGit(t)
691		setTestXDG(t)
692	
693		projectWT, _, _ := makeUpdateRepo(t)
694		// A project-only rule (distinct from makeUpdateRepo's shared no-fri.md) so
695		// its presence in the descendant proves inheritance rather than the write
696		// scope's own copy winning the last-write-wins merge.
697		projectHead := commitFile(t, projectWT, "rules/project-only.md", "Project only.\n", "project rule")
698		projectSrv := serveCanonicalRepo(t, projectWT)
699		dirWT, _, dirHead := makeUpdateRepo(t)
700		dirSrv := serveCanonicalRepo(t, dirWT)
701	
702		// Freeze both scopes: this test isolates the pinned-emission path, not
703		// auto-advance (advancement's revoke gating is covered elsewhere).
704		autoFalse := false
705		home, repo, nested := hierarchyURLTree(t, projectSrv.URL, projectHead, &autoFalse, dirSrv.URL, dirHead, &autoFalse)
706	
707		// Warm the project ancestor's cache by syncing the project scope online
708		// first: descendant runs materialize ancestor layers offline (cache-only),
709		// so without this the project pin is never fetched and never contributes.
710		if _, errOut, err := runSyncHierarchy(t, repo, home); err != nil {
711			t.Fatalf("warm-up project sync: %v\nstderr: %s", err, errOut)
712		}
713		if _, errOut, err := runSyncHierarchy(t, nested, home); err != nil {

go build ./... && go test ./internal/cli/ -run 'TestRunHierarchySync_Refuses' -count=1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "bug#1 tests pass" || echo "BUILD/TEST FAIL"
git add CHANGELOG.md internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go internal/cli/hierarchy_sync_test.go
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(sync): refuse rather than silently delete inherited content when an ancestor layer fails

#43 reshaped hierarchy sync so a run writes one selected scope with ancestor
manifests merged in as read-only layers. When an ancestor layer failed to
materialize this run (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the
offline-forced ancestor cache), its contribution silently dropped out of the
write scope's resolved node set and the engine treated the previously-inherited
paths as orphan deletions — deleting inherited content and exiting 0.

The write scope now dry-runs with engine.Plan when any ancestor it inherits from
failed to materialize, and refuses (non-zero, explanatory error; nothing written
or deleted) when proceeding would delete already-managed content OR the plan
cannot be computed. A fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually
contributed, still syncs its own content unaffected.

engine.Plan records per-target failures in TargetChange.Error and still returns
a nil top-level error, and several of those failures return before planTarget's
deletion loop (leaving WouldDelete empty). The guard therefore treats any
per-target plan error as "cannot prove it is safe" and refuses, so the bug
cannot slip through a plan that errored before counting deletions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git log --oneline -1

input.command:
cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync
gh pr create --title "fix(sync): preserve inherited content when an ancestor layer fails (#43 follow-ups)" --base main --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Resolves the two pre-existing #43 harness-hierarchy inheritance follow-ups documented in the #44 handoff (`docs/handoffs/2026-07-08-001-inline-auto-update-handoff.md`). Both affect `main` independent of the auto-update work.

## Bug #1 — silent inherited-content drop on ancestor failure (fixed)

`runHierarchySync` writes one selected scope with ancestor manifests merged in as read-only layers. When an ancestor layer failed to materialize this run — a malformed ancestor manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the **offline-forced** ancestor cache (`materializeLayerReadOnly` forces `offline := URL != ""`, so a fresh/evicted CI cache hits `ErrUnresolvablePinOffline`) — its contribution silently dropped out of the write scope's resolved node set. The engine then treated the previously-inherited paths as orphan deletions and **deleted inherited content while exiting 0**.

**Fix (chosen contract: _refuse only if it'd delete_).** The write scope now dry-runs with `engine.Plan` whenever an ancestor it inherits from failed, and **refuses** — non-zero exit, explanatory error, nothing written or deleted — when proceeding would delete already-managed content. A fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually contributed (e.g. a chronically cold URL-ancestor cache with no legit deletion), still syncs its own content unaffected.

A subtlety, caught by an adversarial review pass: `engine.Plan` records per-target failures in `TargetChange.Error` and still returns a **nil** top-level error, and several of those failures (`loadLedger`, `runAdapter`, dry-merge, the plan-only out-of-band `readHash`) return **before** `planTarget`'s deletion loop — leaving `WouldDelete` empty. A guard that trusted a zero count from such a plan would let the exact bug slip through a different trigger. The guard therefore treats **any** per-target plan error as "cannot prove it is safe" and refuses. Regression-fenced by `TestRunHierarchySync_RefusesWhenPlanCannotBeComputedForFailedAncestor` (verified: fails without the `ch.Error` check, passes with it).

Bound (documented in-code): the guard protects already-managed, ledger-tracked content, which the plan counts identically to sync. Under `--adopt`, sync also deletes newly-adopted *unmanaged* files the plan does not model — ordinary `--adopt` churn, never inherited content — so the plan can only over-count, never under-count, the deletions this guard cares about.

## Bug #2 — revoked ancestor emission (documented, no behavior change)

Chosen contract: a revoked trust anchor gates **advancement / trust-on-first-use**, not emission of already-pinned content — matching the write-scope pinned-emission path, which has never consulted the revocation log either. Pinned by `TestRunHierarchySync_RevokedAncestorStillEmitsPinnedInheritedContent` (characterization test).

## Verification

- `go vet ./...` clean · `go test -race ./...` green · `golangci-lint run` 0 issues
- total coverage 80.5% (floor 80.0%); new funcs `firstFailedAncestor` 83%, `refuseIfWouldDelete` 91%
- Two existing "continues past a failed ancestor" tests keep passing unchanged (fresh-sync-with-failed-ancestor still emits own content); the one test that codified the old delete-on-failure behavior is rewritten to assert preservation + refusal.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

129	**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
130	- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
131	  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
132	  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
133	- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
134	  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
135	  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
136	  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
137	  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
138	  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
139	  agents-md + skill.
140	
141	- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
142	  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
143	  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
144	  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
145	  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
146	  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
147	  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
148	  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.
149	
150	**Cursor/Pi/Codex parity effort (started 2026-07-04):** Audit found the
151	"unsupported" notes were STALE for Cursor (it shipped Agent Skills + flat
152	`.cursor/commands/`), accurate for Codex (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills are
153	by-design; no code — capability-honesty gate forbids forcing them).
154	- **PR #40 (OPEN, branch feat/cursor-skill):** Cursor `skill` → shared
155	  `.agents/skills/` tree (byte-identical SKILL.md, ADV-1 co-owned w/ codex/pi/
156	  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
157	- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
158	  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
159	  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
160	  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
161	  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
162	  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
163	  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
164	  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
165	  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled
166	  trigger): content-aware orphan delete (cross-mode P0 of the existing content-blind
167	  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
168	  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
169	  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
170	- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
171	  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
172	  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
173	  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
174	  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
175	  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
176	  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
177	  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
178	  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
179	  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
180	  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
181	  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.
182	
183	**Next candidates (in order):**
184	1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
185	   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
186	   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
187	   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
188	   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
189	   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
190	   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
191	   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
192	   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
193	   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
194	   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
195	   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
196	   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
197	   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
198	   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
199	   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
200	   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
201	   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
202	   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
203	   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
204	   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
205	   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
206	   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
207	   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
208	   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
209	   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
210	   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
211	   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
212	   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
213	   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
214	   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
215	   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
216	   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
217	   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
218	   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
219	   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
220	   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
221	   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
222	   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
223	   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
224	2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
225	   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
226	3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
227	   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
228	4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
229	   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
230	   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
231	   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
232	   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
233	   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
234	   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
235	   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
236	   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
237	   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
238	   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
239	   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
240	   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
241	   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
242	   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /
243	   golangci-lint all clean. **SHIPPED: PR #39 squash-merged to main (e05def1)
244	   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
245	   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
246	   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
247	   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
248	   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
249	   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.
250	
251	See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].
252	
253	**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
254	dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
255	flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
256	(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
257	`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
258	not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
259	drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
260	ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
261	non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
262	on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
263	plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
264	newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
265	`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
266	chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
267	passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
268	(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
269	rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
270	Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.
271	
272	**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
273	shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
274	rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
275	`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
276	`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
277	incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
278	break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
279	`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
280	Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
281	the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
282	newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).
283	
284	**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
285	(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
286	lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

289	adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.
290	
291	The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
292	+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
293	(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
294	change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].
