---
name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — PR #44 OPEN (inline auto-update: sync advances the pin by default, semver-major, plan 2026-07-08-001); #42 MERGED (e53317b). v0.7.0 released 2026-07-04; next candidates --break-lock and windsurf/lmstudio/roo adapters
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

**PR #44 (MERGED 2026-07-08, squash `63aca44`, branch deleted):** inline auto-update —
`agent-sync sync` advances the canonical pin to newest upstream **by default**
(semver-major posture flip). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-08-001-feat-inline-auto-update-plan.md`,
brainstorm `docs/brainstorms/2026-07-08-inline-auto-update-requirements.md`. 8 units,
6 commits. Opt-outs: `--frozen` flag + `canonical.auto: false` (`*bool`, nil⇒on).
Rails: fast-forward-only; revoke always refuses; offline→cached-pin fallback;
old→new SHA to log + cache audit. Trust routed THROUGH `trust.Decide` via new
`PostureAllowNewSHAs`/`KindProceedAutoAdvance` — NOT bypassed; new `DecideInput.StateLoaded`
makes the posture fail-closed without a folded store (revoke stays load-bearing).
New `git.Input.ResolveOrFallback` materialize mode; shared `resolveAdvance`/`repinManifest`
extracted from `update` (`internal/cli/advance.go`); per-scope wiring in
`prepareScopeForSync`/`prepareAutoAdvanceScope` (`setup.go`), run-lock held across
re-pin+emit (RunLockHeld=true), hierarchy stays continue-and-report, pin-moved=exit 6.
Threat model T1 + CHANGELOG (breaking + migration) + README updated. **Implemented via
Codex delegation (`gpt-5.4`, ce-work-beta); trust hardening + 3 post-review fixes + docs
authored in Claude Code.** TWO adversarial review passes: pre-activation caught the
caller-contingent revoke defense (→ StateLoaded guard, commit 30fa697); post-activation
caught a TOCTOU ff-baseline (→ reload manifest under lock), an empty-`trusted_sha`
divergence (→ require trusted_sha for eligibility), and a routing-predicate coupling
(→ gate on !local_path) — all fixed in 8f1af84. Full `-race` gate + lint green.
local_path is ff-gated but NOT trust-gated (consistent w/ materializeLocal; revoke keyed
by URL). See [[spend-limit-degrades-review]], [[ci-darwin-amd64-runner-stall]].
**Merge integration (main had advanced past #42 to #43 harness-hierarchy):** #43 flipped
hierarchy sync to a SINGLE selected write scope + read-only ancestor LAYERS
(selectWriteScopes emits one; materializeLayerReadOnly + applyResolvedLayers merge inherited
nodes) and added LevelWorkspace + scope-aware decodeAt/applyCursorComposition signatures.
Auto-advance now applies to the single write scope; verified the approved SHA survives
applyResolvedLayers (it only rewrites req.Nodes; write scope's layer merges last/wins).
--workspace single-scope path also auto-advances (runSingleScopeSync → prepareScopeForSync).
TWO adversarial-review-flagged **pre-existing #43 inheritance bugs** left as follow-ups (NOT
fixed in #44, on main regardless): (1) transient ancestor-layer read failure silently drops
inherited content from descendant (no D8-style guard); (2) revoked ANCESTOR source still
emits inherited content (read-only layers use materializeURL pinned path, no revocation
consult). **CI gotchas hit + fixed:** git-daemon `--pid-file` read raced the async detach
write (flaky "read git daemon pid: no such file" under -race+coverage) → poll for pidfile;
`git daemon` unavailable on Windows CI → skip serveCanonicalRepo URL tests on GOOS=windows
(covered on unix + daemon-free local_path tests). Coverage floor 80.0%, razor-thin ~80.5%.
Merged via `gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` (branch protection REVIEW_REQUIRED;
darwin/amd64 passed this time). 0.8.0 (#42/#43) + auto-update sit unreleased on main.

**PR #42 (MERGED `e53317b`, branch `feat/init-defaults-user-sync-ux`):** zero-flag
`init` — source defaults to `.agents` (created via fsroot), targets discovered
from bundled adapters' `ReservedPrefix` dirs (exact-stat, `.agents` never
matches antigravity's `.agent`), zero-match writes empty targets + hint,
success line announces inferences, wizard empty-Enter default + discovery
preselection, `--target` now skips the wizard; plus sync user-scope offer
([Y/n] prompt via `hierarchySyncOptions.OfferUser` callback seam) and a
persistent skipped-user `notice` (text + JSON). Also fixed the duplicated
`<name>: started` stderr lines: adapterkit session banner (subprocess
proof-of-life) leaking from bundled in-process adapters — now `io.Discard`
in all five `bundled.go` files. Plan
`docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-feat-init-defaults-target-discovery-plan.md`
(R1–R18); master plan Unit 17 annotated (partial delivery; full branching
wizard + `docs/wizard/init-states.md` still open). Behavior changes: bare
`init --non-interactive` succeeds now; pin flags without source → named
error. Full gate green (36 pkgs -race, lint 0, GOOS=windows vet). User's
installed binary was v0.4.0 from /usr/local/bin — remind to reinstall after
release. 6 commits, TDD throughout.

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).

**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
Antigravity CLI adapter.

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Cursor/Pi/Codex parity effort (started 2026-07-04):** Audit found the
"unsupported" notes were STALE for Cursor (it shipped Agent Skills + flat
`.cursor/commands/`), accurate for Codex (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills are
by-design; no code — capability-honesty gate forbids forcing them).
- **PR #40 (OPEN, branch feat/cursor-skill):** Cursor `skill` → shared
  `.agents/skills/` tree (byte-identical SKILL.md, ADV-1 co-owned w/ codex/pi/
  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled
  trigger): content-aware orphan delete (cross-mode P0 of the existing content-blind
  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /
   golangci-lint all clean. **SHIPPED: PR #39 squash-merged to main (e05def1)
   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`

input.new_string:
TWO adversarial-review-flagged **pre-existing #43 inheritance bugs** left as follow-ups (NOT
fixed in #44, on main regardless): (1) transient ancestor-layer read failure silently drops
inherited content from descendant (no D8-style guard); (2) revoked ANCESTOR source still
emits inherited content (read-only layers use materializeURL pinned path, no revocation
consult). **BOTH RESOLVED in PR #45 (OPEN, branch `fix/hierarchy-inheritance-followups`,
commits 08d4c6c fix + 509dc14 test):** chosen contracts (via AskUserQuestion) —
(1) *refuse only if it'd delete*: write scope dry-runs `engine.Plan` when a `firstFailedAncestor`
exists and refuses (non-zero, nothing written/deleted) if `WouldDelete>0` OR any per-target
`TargetChange.Error` (engine.Plan swallows per-target errors into a nil top-level return, and
several return before planTarget's deletion loop → empty WouldDelete would falsely pass;
adversarial review caught this — `refuseIfWouldDelete` treats any ch.Error as refuse);
`refuseIfWouldDelete`+`firstFailedAncestor` in hierarchy_sync.go; fresh/never-contributed
ancestor still emits own content. (2) *advancement/TOFU-only* — no behavior change, revoke
gates advance not pinned emission; characterization test only. Ledger has NO per-path source
provenance (Entry={Path,SHA256,Size,EmittedAt}) so deletion can't be attributed to the dropped
ancestor — errs toward refusing. `--adopt` bound documented (plan only over-counts inherited
deletions). Coverage 80.5%. Adversarial review by ce-adversarial-reviewer caught the
engine.Plan-swallows-per-target-error gap (finding #1, high) → fixed + fenced by
TestRunHierarchySync_RefusesWhenPlanCannotBeComputedForFailedAncestor.

content.text:
Done. Both #43 inheritance follow-ups from the handoff are implemented, adversarially reviewed, and shipped to a PR — stopped before merge as you chose.

## What landed on PR #45

**https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/pull/45** (branch `fix/hierarchy-inheritance-followups`, 2 atomic commits)

- **`08d4c6c` — bug #1 fix.** When an ancestor layer fails to materialize, the write scope now dry-runs with `engine.Plan` and **refuses** (non-zero exit, explanatory error, nothing written/deleted) if proceeding would delete already-managed content or the plan can't be computed. Fresh/never-contributing ancestors still sync their own content. Implements your *refuse-only-if-it'd-delete* choice.
- **`509dc14` — bug #2 contract test.** Pins your *advancement/TOFU-only* choice: a revoked anchor gates advancement, not pinned emission. No behavior change.

## The adversarial pass earned its keep

It found a genuine high-severity hole in my first cut: `engine.Plan` swallows per-target failures into `TargetChange.Error` and returns a nil top-level error, and several of those failures return *before* `planTarget`'s deletion loop (empty `WouldDelete`). My guard would have proceeded on that deceptively-zero count and let the exact bug through a different trigger. Fixed (any per-target plan error → refuse) and fenced with a regression test I verified fails without the fix. Finding #2 (`--adopt` divergence) traced to not touch inherited content — documented in-code.

## Verification (all green)
`go vet` clean · `go test -race ./...` green · `golangci-lint` 0 issues · coverage 80.5% (floor 80.0%) · CHANGELOG stamped.

## Notes for you
- **Not merged** — ready for your review; merge when you're satisfied (the previous rhythm was `gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch`). Watch out for the chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` runner stall — infra, not code.
- The previous session's handoff doc (`docs/handoffs/2026-07-08-001-...`) is still untracked in your working tree; I left it alone.
- **Release chore still pending** (unchanged): `0.8.0` + inline auto-update + these fixes all sit unreleased on `main` — a semver-major cut (stamp CHANGELOG + tag) is the next non-feature task.
On it: merge #45 → release → local cleanup. First let me check the PR's CI status and the actual release state (tags, CHANGELOG) before merging and picking a version.

content.content:
1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.
4	
5	The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
6	and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7	
8	While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
9	the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
10	compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.
11	
12	## [Unreleased]
13	
14	### Fixed
15	
16	- **Hierarchy sync no longer silently deletes inherited content when an ancestor
17	  layer can't be materialized.** When a read-only ancestor scope failed to
18	  materialize this run (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the
19	  offline-forced ancestor cache), its contribution silently dropped out of the
20	  write scope's resolved set and the engine deleted the previously-inherited
21	  paths (exit 0). The write scope now dry-runs first and **refuses** — preserving
22	  the inherited files and surfacing a non-zero, explanatory error — whenever
23	  proceeding would delete already-managed content or the plan can't be computed.
24	  A fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually contributed, still syncs its
25	  own content unaffected. (#43 inheritance follow-up.)
26	
27	### Changed
28	
29	- **BREAKING (semver-major): `agent-sync sync` auto-advances the canonical pin by
30	  default.** Previously `sync` materialized the pinned `commit` and never moved it;
31	  advancing required the explicit, gated `agent-sync update`. Now `sync` resolves
32	  the newest upstream commit on the manifest's `ref`, and — for git-backed sources
33	  that are pinned and carry a `trusted_sha` — advances the pin and re-pins
34	  `commit` + `trusted_sha` forward automatically, auto-accepting the trust
35	  decision **through the trust policy engine**. Safety rails: advancement is
36	  **fast-forward-only** (rewritten/force-pushed history is refused, pin kept); a
37	  **revoked trust anchor always refuses**; **offline / unreachable falls back to
38	  the cached pin** with a warning (never errors); every advance is recorded to the
39	  structured log and the cache audit file. `local_dir` sources are unaffected
40	  (nothing to pin); `local_path` sources advance fast-forward-only. Applies per
41	  scope across the user → workspace → project/directory hierarchy.
42	
43	  **Migration.** On upgrade, existing pinned workspaces begin tracking upstream on
44	  the next `sync`. To keep today's deterministic, human-gated behavior:
45	  - pass `--frozen` for a single run, or
46	  - set `canonical.auto: false` in `.agent-sync.yaml` to opt a workspace out
47	    permanently (then advance deliberately with `agent-sync update`).
48	
49	  CI and any reproducibility-sensitive pipeline should use one of the opt-outs.
50	  See `docs/threat-model.md` (T1) for the revised supply-chain posture: human
51	  review is no longer in the default advance path, so fast-forward-only and the
52	  trust policy are the remaining rails, and commit-signature verification is still
53	  absent.
54	
55	## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-07

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [Unreleased]

### Fixed

- **Hierarchy sync no longer silently deletes inherited content when an ancestor
  layer can't be materialized.** When a read-only ancestor scope failed to
  materialize this run (malformed manifest, or a git-backed pin absent from the
  offline-forced ancestor cache), its contribution silently dropped out of the
  write scope's resolved set and the engine deleted the previously-inherited
  paths (exit 0). The write scope now dry-runs first and **refuses** — preserving
  the inherited files and surfacing a non-zero, explanatory error — whenever
  proceeding would delete already-managed content or the plan can't be computed.
  A fresh scope, or an ancestor that never actually contributed, still syncs its
  own content unaffected. (#43 inheritance follow-up.)

### Changed

- **BREAKING (semver-major): `agent-sync sync` auto-advances the canonical pin by
  default.** Previously `sync` materialized the pinned `commit` and never moved it;
  advancing required the explicit, gated `agent-sync update`. Now `sync` resolves
  the newest upstream commit on the manifest's `ref`, and — for git-backed sources
  that are pinned and carry a `trusted_sha` — advances the pin and re-pins
  `commit` + `trusted_sha` forward automatically, auto-accepting the trust
  decision **through the trust policy engine**. Safety rails: advancement is
  **fast-forward-only** (rewritten/force-pushed history is refused, pin kept); a
  **revoked trust anchor always refuses**; **offline / unreachable falls back to
  the cached pin** with a warning (never errors); every advance is recorded to the
  structured log and the cache audit file. `local_dir` sources are unaffected
  (nothing to pin); `local_path` sources advance fast-forward-only. Applies per
  scope across the user → workspace → project/directory hierarchy.

  **Migration.** On upgrade, existing pinned workspaces begin tracking upstream on
  the next `sync`. To keep today's deterministic, human-gated behavior:
  - pass `--frozen` for a single run, or
  - set `canonical.auto: false` in `.agent-sync.yaml` to opt a workspace out
    permanently (then advance deliberately with `agent-sync update`).

  CI and any reproducibility-sensitive pipeline should use one of the opt-outs.
  See `docs/threat-model.md` (T1) for the revised supply-chain posture: human
  review is no longer in the default advance path, so fast-forward-only and the
  trust policy are the remaining rails, and commit-signature verification is still
  absent.

## [0.8.0] - 2026-07-07

content.content:
=== last 20 lines (link refs?) ===
  linux/arm64, windows/amd64) with SHA-256 checksums and optional minisign
  signatures.
- CI coverage gate: total statement coverage is enforced against the 80% floor
  mandated by CLAUDE.md and fails the build on a drop. Added behavioral tests
  for previously-untested packages (`internal/validate`, `conformance/echo`,
  `cmd/agent-sync`) plus the adapter op round-trip, watch sync, trust store,
  and conformance assertion paths to clear it.
- Fixed a nil-pointer panic in `adapterkit.ExitError.Error()` (the `e == nil`
  guard dereferenced `e.Code` in the same branch), surfaced by the new tests.
- CI now fails loud if the real-git/real-filesystem end-to-end tests are
  skipped, via `AGENT_SYNC_REQUIRE_GIT=1`.

### Changed

- `README` tool-support table now distinguishes bundled adapters (Claude Code,
  Cursor, Codex CLI) from planned ones, instead of advertising unimplemented tiers.

### Removed

- Roo Code from the planned tool list (decommissioned upstream).
=== grep compare links ===
